conflicts.description: The prior continuity sheet treated Marisol's old restraint-death cleanup as something Gabriel learned in chapter 13, but chapter 13 states he already knew the lie as a teenager and later saw the Paper House packet before the novel begins.
conflicts.canonical_value: Gabriel knew before story start that Marisol had helped conceal an old restraint death; chapter 13 is the full confession and confirmation.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter 13 itself supplies two pre-story sources for Gabriel's knowledge, so the sheet was updated to reflect pre-story knowledge rather than a chapter-13 discovery.

conflicts.description: Naila's missing fingernails are described on the right hand in chapters 5 and 12 but on the left hand in chapters 8 and 10.
conflicts.canonical_value: Naila is missing two fingernails on her left hand.
conflicts.resolution_note: The left-hand version recurs through the refusal and planning chapters and is tied to her counting and grip actions; the right-hand version appears as brief descriptive drift.

conflicts.description: Ivo's daughter is named Lina in early scenes, but chapter 14's narration calls her Mina once.
conflicts.resolution_note: Lina is named twice in-scene and remains the same child linked to Nora and the marsh-hawk birthday memory; Mina is a one-off narration slip.
